what_it_does: This file is a coordination brief written by the cognition session to align the convergence/interface session and the guided-review session on ownership, overlapping code areas, co-design touchpoints, sequencing, and mutual responsibilities while building the cognition engine and its consumers.
observations: The cognition session claims ownership of the Concurrent Cognition layer: runtime/cognition.py, runtime/roles.py, roles/*, runtime/rules.py, runtime/activation.py, the CognitionView, runtime/authoring.py, the /api/cognition/* routes, contracts/cognition_info.py, and ops/cli/capabilities.py.

The cognition session's current active build is labeled B/C/A: mode reach-extension, engine generalization (run_role input becomes declared addressed values / input_addresses and run_items axis-inversion), and a new cross-unit REDUCE.
The file explicitly maps where the guided-review build overlaps cognition territory: roles/, activation.py, MODE_REGISTRY, and bridge.py voice stream.
It asks the guided-review session for an overlap map distinguishing net-new work from reuse of cognition-owned code.
It reconciles an identity question: the guided-review session is the same lineage as the convergence/interface session, so there is one owner of the review/guided-review surface.
Three co-design touchpoints are identified: walkthrough roles/screen_reader ↔ engine generalization C; cast_posture axis on MODE_REGISTRY ↔ A; mockup:// scheme is already cognition's to fold.
The file frames three complementary perspectives: coherence/interface sees structure, guided-review sees the operator seat, and cognition sees the engine from inside.
It states engine laws that cognition guards: operator-only floor, demote-only, reflects-never-owns, and closed rule grammar.
Coordination mechanics are referenced: WORK-SPLIT.md CLAIMS board, MERGE-COORDINATION.md message relay, file-disjoint editing, and green company suites before shared commits.
The authoring backend is described as UI-less by design, with 12 /api/cognition/* endpoints intended as a Claude Design target.
standouts: The engine generalization (C) is positioned as the shared keystone, with guided-review's walkthrough as its first consumer.
The identity reconciliation prevents a 'built twice' drift between convergence/interface and guided-review.
Cognition offers to be the single source of truth for swarm-engine capabilities and to catch designs that violate engine laws.
conventions: Written in first-person session voice, signing as 'cognition'.
Uses cross-references to other coordination files: WORK-SPLIT.md, MERGE-COORDINATION.md, MODE-SYSTEM-MAP.md, build-prep/concurrent-cognition/.
Employs work-package labels B/C/A without defining them in this file.
Uses a claim-before-edit protocol ('claim shared files in WORK-SPLIT.md § CLAIMS').
Structures content as ownership statement, asks, reconciliation, touchpoints, sequencing, and mutual-help framework.
concerns: The file is not self-contained: it relies heavily on external files and labels that are not reproduced here.
It asserts unresolved or underspecified states, such as the 'always-on driver' being undriven and the exact shape of the screen_reader role.
It carries an ownership-tension tone ('built twice', 'one owner') that may reflect coordination risk rather than technical risk.
Several key terms (operator-only floor, demote-only, reflects-never-owns, closed rule grammar) are invoked but not defined locally.
notes: A session-authored coordination brief that maps who owns the cognition engine, where the guided-review surface intersects it, and how the three sessions should co-design rather than collide.
questions: What are the exact contents of the B, C, and A work packages?
What is the structure and current state of the CLAIMS board in WORK-SPLIT.md?
What are the 13 mode axes referenced in the mode-dial reach?
What is the precise definition and current implementation of the operator-only floor, demote-only, reflects-never-owns, and closed rule grammar?
What is the screen_reader role's intended shape and injection edge?
What is the mockup:// scheme and the studio-suite-mockup-focus.patch?
What is the bridge.py:848 focus-passthrough fix?
What are the 12 /api/cognition/* routes and their current UI-less status?
What is the always-on driver in runtime/activation.py and why is it undriven?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a brief for the guided-review session and the shared record; the actual content matches that purpose by coordinating ownership, overlap, asks, reconciliation, and sequencing.
apparent_themes: Build once, consume many: a single generalized engine should serve cognition, coherence detectors, corpus work, and guided-review.
Ownership clarity prevents duplication: one named owner per surface, claims board for shared files.
Additive composition over reinvention: guided-review reuses existing engine parts.
Three-perspective triangulation: structure, operator experience, and engine internals must align.
Engine laws are guarded from inside the cognition layer.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish the cognition session as the authoritative source for swarm-engine design, fold the guided-review consumer requirements into the engine generalization, and prevent parallel construction of the same surfaces by clarifying identities and claims.
novelty: It is an unusually meta coordination artifact: one build session writes a brief to peer sessions, performs explicit identity reconciliation, and proposes a claims-board protocol for concurrent file editing.
contribution: It contributes a clear ownership map and co-design contract for the cognition engine and its consumers, identifies the engine generalization as a shared seam, and records the resolution of the convergence/guided-review identity question.
summary_for_embedding: A coordination brief from the cognition session that defines ownership of the Concurrent Cognition layer, maps overlaps with the guided-review surface, reconciles session identity, and proposes co-design touchpoints and a claims-based editing protocol.
intention_for_embedding: To align the convergence/interface and guided-review sessions with the cognition session's live engine work so that overlapping files are edited disjointly and the engine generalization serves all consumers.
